subject: Configure Jacoco plugin to enforce min coverage
commit_message:
Configure Jacoco plugin to enforce min coverage

Jacoco plugin is configured to set the minimum line coverage ratios to
equal that of the current coverage level, to prevent future updates from
decreasing the coverage level.

Change-Id: I5913c3bf99fd98e344eaf6ee176e232bd7e61bb1
Issue-ID: AAI-2293
Signed-off-by: Lee, Tian (tl5884) <tianl@amdocs.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/aai-event-client-master-verify-java/46/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/aai-event-client-master-verify-java/46

Patch Set 1:

A few questions ...
Do we need coverage limits on the sub-projects?  Or if we put a single coverage limit on the top-level pom will that include code fro m the subprojects?

I'm a bit concerned that you've set some of the sub-project limits to 100%.  That doesn't give us any wiggle room if something is added that isn't easily unit testable.  

Wondering if it would make sense to set these to like 90%?  Or possibly just set limits on the top-level pom to whatever we are at now?

Patch Set 1:

> A few questions ...
 > Do we need coverage limits on the sub-projects?  Or if we put a
 > single coverage limit on the top-level pom will that include code
 > fro m the subprojects?
 > 
 > I'm a bit concerned that you've set some of the sub-project limits
 > to 100%.  That doesn't give us any wiggle room if something is
 > added that isn't easily unit testable.
 > 
 > Wondering if it would make sense to set these to like 90%?  Or
 > possibly just set limits on the top-level pom to whatever we are at
 > now?

Having a global limit for all sub projects means that we need to set it to the "lowest common denominator", which in this case is rabbit-mq, at ~65%. This means we cannot effectively enforce the no-drop policy of the other sub projects, which have much higher code coverage.

However, I'm fine with having a ceiling for the coverage value at 90%. I can make this amendment.
Patch Set 2: Published edit on patch set 1.
